Okay, this next chunk is called the three walls. The three walls. Now we did a test in the first part of this session called body language. I want to see how many of you passed the test and how many of you failed the test. A timeline begins, then it ends. You should have noticed a problem with that. So we're going to shift it to see if you pick up on it. To you, the beginning should be over here, shouldn't it? So the beginning should be here and your clothes should be where? Now how many of you picked up on that earlier and said congratulations you passed the test? What that really means is I failed the test. Because I'm looking at my left or my right. So I have to understand that this is your left and this is right. So from now on, y'all help me. This is the clothes in the timeline, alright? Will that help you understand it a little bit better? Yes ma'am? I'm sorry to have this question. But isn't it the whole thing that you think of as the short circuit that you use thinking to...I put a recession in the middle of your house and I just took a short circuit. I don't know. I just thought it was a good idea that I reversed it so that it made more sense to you. You had a quick comment? Well the reality is if you're beginning with the end in mind it doesn't need you. Yeah, okay. It's a non-issue one way or the other. This is called the walls. The walls. In teaching this chunk, it is one of the chunks that I consider absolutely essential that you have in every sales presentation. Some length of it. I'll give you the long length and then you can shorten it to whatever degree you feel is appropriate. But the walls is appropriate. Now what you will do, you know, I am not addressing your content. Have you noticed that? See, I don't know what your topic is. What I am showing you in a sales presentation, there is a shell. And the shell starts with the things I've given you. The shell will start with financial independence or a picture of where they want to go. And it starts with a promise of what they will learn here. Tell them what you are going to tell them. And then in the thing, the shell, you'll insert testimonials in here. Does a testimonial have a format? Please say yes. Okay. Now that testimonial will verify everything that you are saying, but subliminally the testimonial tells them to come to your training or to buy your product. That's the key to the testimonial formula that I gave you. There is no limit to how many of those you should have in your presentation. As many as you can plug in there. I'm going to give you a word that you can feel very comfortable about teaching anything. The word is we, because this is what happens. In the beginning when you are speaking, you got two choices. Either get up there and act like you have no credibility because you have no credibility or lie. Because how can you get up there and say, I'm a millionaire if you're not a millionaire? Could you now get up and say, let me tell you about our net worth. Let me tell you what we put together. Who's we? Are you a part of a mastermind team? Are you part of a team that when you add all the values of the net worth, are we worth several million dollars here? Okay. It will build credibility. It allows you to speak freely now. Because you have said we. Let me tell you what we did. You may not have ever did it. You may not have ever done this. I'm from Mississippi. But I teach no strategies that I haven't done. When I started becoming a speaker, I went to every seminar. When I started investing, I went to everything I could do. Everything I could go to. And then I would do those strategies just for the experience. I didn't get to be really good at any of the strategies because I only did them normally once because I wanted the experience. I was going to be an instructor. Okay. So as I grew though, the ones that were really powerful, I did over and over again. And I learned that there's not a whole lot of difference between let me tell you what I did versus let me tell you what we did. And you can feel very comfortable with using the word we. We can be anybody, you and your parents, you and your partner, you and I, whatever it is, it's a word that allows you to feel comfortable building your credibility in front of an audience. In your content, you need to have what I call case studies. When you make a point, you say, let's just do a case study on that. Okay. So I just a point in which I said, you can actually double your money on a piece of real estate. Let's do a case study and see how that does. And then you can insert some example of what we did and you can teach from that case study. I'm not going to do a case study for you. I'm leaving that up to you. Now sometime in your case study, I would like for you to run into a wall. Oh, I see. I could make 25,000, but I have to have the 25,000. Oh, I see. You'll give me a good deal, but I have to have $600. Bam. Did I run into a wall? Okay. When you run into the wall in your case study, what do you do? Do you run a voice, body language? By the way, will we always be facing situations in which we run into walls? Well, let me share with you that there are three walls that you will be facing in life. Wall number one is lack of knowledge. You teach a case study and people look up and say, well, I have to do that. If I could do that, yeah. If I knew how to do that, I'd do it. But if you don't know how to do that, you can't do it. Lack of knowledge. Will that stop you? Could stop you. If you don't know how to do it, it'll stop you, won't it? Okay. Here's another wall. I see how that's done. I think I can do that. It's just that I'm just afraid. I am scared to death. It's called fear. Actually, it's called lack of confidence. Can that stop you in life? Absolutely. Lack of knowledge can stop you. Lack of confidence can stop you. And the last wall is called lack of money. Well, John, the rich get richer and the poor get poorer. Hey, if I had a lot of money, yeah, I could be wealthy. But I don't have a lot of money, so I can't become wealthy. Is that the decision that an untrained mind makes? By the way, how many of you were told that it takes money to make money? Okay, is that true? No, that's not true. I'm a multimillionaire, and I'm going to tell you, it takes money to make money. Well, that's not what I wanted to hear. All my life I hear it takes money to make money. Now a multimillionaire tells you it takes money to make money, and I don't have any money. I put my hand in my pocket, I got lent. I don't know what chance you got. It's called a half-truth. There are half-truths that absolutely stagnate our careers. How many of you would like to know the whole truth about this? Yes, it does take money to make money. However, it doesn't have to be your money. When I got started, I needed $600. The seller says, here's the deal, you have to put up $600. That's not going to change. Bam! I ran into the wall called lack of money, but I called someone. Financially, they said, well, for this price, you won't have the problem. Okay, let's go through these walls. By the way, do you have to have all three walls to stop you? All it takes is one wall to stop you, doesn't it? Do you think understanding how to... Okay, so by the way, have you ever noticed that everything you want financially seems to be on which side of the wall? Always on the other side of the wall, isn't it? You tell me then. How are you going to get on the other side of the wall? You going through it? Round it, under it, over it, tear it down, sell it, lease it, put a window in it. They got to go, folks. Walls have got to go. Because the reality is, we will be facing the walls. The difference between successful people in this life are not whether or not they face the same walls that everyone else did. The secret is successful people simply learn how to handle the walls. Let's go through them one by one. Lack of knowledge. Are we talking about things today that have power or worth a lot of money? It's good information, good knowledge. Where do you get knowledge like this? Let's talk about all the places that we've been taught to go get knowledge. Schools, colleges, university. Where else do you go to get knowledge? Libraries. Where else? Go the internet. Go the street. What's it called? Trial and error. Right? Trial and terror. I'm sorry. You learn by doing. Learn from a mentor. Where else? Right in the back of the room. Right in the back of the room. Home study courses, right? Books. In this part of your presentation, let your audience tell you everything that they want to tell you about where you can overcome lack of knowledge. And you're going to have to address every one of them. Let's talk about colleges and universities. How many of you went to college or university? Okay? Remember that course you took called How to Make Money 101? It wasn't there, was it? Now see, we had been taught, you want to make a lot of money? Go to college. You get to college and they don't teach you how to make money. They teach you how to have a good liberal arts education and how you can go out and get yourself a good what? Job. Well, we already found jobs don't take us where we want to be. We're going to have to get into the results for money. That'd be more like an entrepreneurial school than it is a scholastic college or university. Okay? So you can't go there to get this kind of knowledge. Somebody mentioned you, well, why don't you just read a book? Okay? How many of you have read good books on making money? Can you read a book and become wealthy? You can. But how often does it happen? It seems like, yes, you can get good information but there's something about books. Did you know if you could read a book, become wealthy, the highest paid profession in the world would be librarians? Are they the highest paid? They have got full access to all the information in the world right there in the books, right at your disposal. Are they the highest paid? No. No, there must be a problem with books. How about home study courses? Can you learn how to become wealthy with a home study course? Yeah, you can. How many of you have home study courses, by the way? How many of you have home study courses that have never been opened? Can I see a hands on your hand? Oh. You know, I was selling a home study course one time called The Lazy Way to Buy Real Estate. And my tape duplicator called me one time and said, John, we've got a major problem. I said, what is it? They said, tape number five on your home study course is blank. God, how in the world did that happen? I said, well, don't know. We just, somehow or another, a blank tape got in there as the master. We've just been duplicating a blank tape onto a blank tape. I'm thinking, well, it's not that much of a problem because I have a written guarantee. If you have any problem with any cassettes, you just call this 800 number, we'll send you a new tape number five or whatever it is. Out of 1,000 people that bought that home study course, how many people do you think call to get a new tape number five? Seven. What does that tell you? I don't know, but they didn't get the tape number five, did they? And I think one of the reasons for that is that at the end of tape number four, I say, now take what you've learned at this point and you go out and apply it. You learn by doing. The trial, the errors, the making mistakes out here. Okay, when people got out there and they run into a blank, this stuff doesn't work, right? Can you learn by trial and error? You can. Now, can you learn by your mistakes? My father taught me that. Son, you need to go out and learn by your mistakes. A bunch of crap is what it is. Let me show you the reality of what you've just been taught. I got two books in my hand. When you make a mistake, all you do is learn other things that do not work. I got two books in my hand. Here's a book of all the things that do not work. Here's a book of all the things that do work. Which book would you rather have? The book of things that work. And yet society teaches, no, you need to learn more things that don't work. You need to learn by your mistakes. It's a bunch of crap. How many of you heard that practice makes perfect? How many of you have told that? It's a lie! Perfect practice makes perfect. What would happen if you practiced the wrong thing financially about making money? Over and over again you did the wrong thing. And pretty soon without even thinking you do it how? Wrong. Now let me tell you. If you were doing the right process to become wealthy, what result would you get? The wealthy result. Now if you were doing the process that does not take you to become wealthy and if you kept doing that process, what happens? Remember how Einstein says insanity is continuing to do what you've always done but you expect to get different results. If you continue to implement the financial strategies you have been implementing all your life, where will you be in five years? Right where you are. Once again your audience needs to understand they have to change and you're showing them some half truths, some principles that we've all been taught that aren't necessarily the truth. Perfect practice makes perfect. So let me show you an example of what I'm talking about. Can you remember the first time you ever drove an automobile? Was that a traumatic experience or what? First time you ever drove an automobile? I remember very clearly. I got in that car, you know, where does the key go? You search and you finally put it in there and well how do you start it? Well you've got to turn the key on. Turn the key on, nothing happened. I thought how do you start it? No you've got to turn the key over to the starter. No you've got to push the clutch in. Push the clutch in, then you start it. Now do you remember that terrible noise it made when you didn't know you were supposed to let off on the starter? See what happens, certain things you go through you have to actually give everything a conscious thought. And then well how do I make this thing go? You've got to put it in gear. You push the clutch in, you put it in gear, nothing happens. Why didn't it go? You've got to let the clutch out. Okay boom, you know and off you go, right? And they didn't say to ease it out. And off you go, down you're heading down the driveway, oh my goodness going down there. What would have happened if somebody walked out in front of you? Now let's see, I should first, you know, boom, you're one dead duck there, right? Now how many of you drove your car here or somewhere in the last week or so? Different experience wasn't it? Did you know I'll bet you anything by feel you put the key to the right spot? You started it and never gave a thought to the fact of letting off that key, just automatically did that. Put it in reverse, backed out, put it in drive, just easing out of the driveway, going along there, get up on a freeway, you're tooling down there, going about, you know, whatever your speed is, and then all of a sudden somebody put it over it without even thinking, you let off on an accelerator which is contrary to Las Vegas driving I noticed. And you avoided the problem. Now I want to know this, how did you develop the ability to make literally thousands and thousands of correct decisions without giving it a conscious thought? It's called perfect practice. Let me ask you a question. How many of you have noticed that I ask a lot of questions? Now are my questions difficult? Do you know the answer to most of my questions? Don't you think I know that you know the answer? Well if I know you know the answer, why do you think I ask you the question? Because I'm a teacher and I know that you need to become decisive in nature. The only way you can become decisive in nature is when you start making correct decisions over and over and over and some of you refuse to practice. You refuse to answer a simple question. It's perfect practice. You will never ever become decisive in nature if you don't start making correct decisions. And correct decisions are the little ones, the simple ones. But if you said you're not practicing, you must develop the ability to become decisive in nature. Lack of knowledge. Did you know that there's one method of learning that has never failed? You know we've listed a lot of things that do fail. Sounds like good to get a home study course, get a good book, go to college, go to university, get a mentor. There's been one method of learning that has never failed and always works. By the way, if you are about to make a decision on whether or not to learn to become wealthy, is that a major decision for you? Okay, do you want to make the correct decision? Are you wanting something that might work or something that has never failed? Never failed. Then you need to understand the method of learning. There is one method that has never failed and that's one a craftsman takes an apprentice and they tell him how to do it and then they show him how to do it. Where do you learn the most? Being told or shown? Shown. But that's only two of the three steps. The craftsman tells you how to do it, they show you how to do it and then they make you do it. Do they want you to learn by your mistakes? No. When I take a student, I tell them how to do it, I show them how to do it and I stand over them and watch them and make them practice it perfectly. Perfect practice makes perfect. And I guarantee you, each and every one of you that has a job right now, that's a job you know how to do. I bet you anything, the method of learning you went through was you came in there, someone told you how to do it, showed you how to do it and kind of sat next to you while you did it. And they didn't like you making a mistake. Every time you made a mistake they pointed it out to you, didn't they? And pretty soon because you were doing it over and over again the correct way you can now do your job very competently and just like an expert. There's a method of learning that we need to learn. Now let me ask you this. I used to teach a home study course. I used to do a boot camp on teaching people how to invest in real estate. Do you think it would make a major difference in your investing career if you had a mentor that told you, showed you and watched you while you did it? How many of you think you could raise the bar and the yield on your investment if you went through that type of learning process? Can I see a show of hands on that? So there is a way, lack of knowledge. What would happen to your knowledge if you learned that way? What happens to that wall? It's gone. Now let me ask you another thing. When you are doing something perfectly, in the beginning you did it perfectly because I told you to do it and I didn't want you to make a mistake. I made you do it right. Now when you do it again and again and again, pretty soon you're doing it perfectly because it's automatic. Now I want to know this. What happens to your confidence level now? It goes up. So you overcame lack of confidence with what? Knowledge. Ah, in other words knowledge is the answer to both of those walls, isn't it? Well let's go on to the third wall, lack of money. You got this case study. Here this is a money making opportunity. If you only put up this amount of money you could get involved. You know it's a good deal. You had this is your opportunity and you're going to act but it's going to take some money. Bam! You just ran into a wall. I don't have your money. It's not the same wall I had. How about if you could pay $600, we'd help you finance the property. Now where did I get the $600? I got a partner. If you knew the strategy of win-win, there's not a strategy of win-win. There are 52 strategies of win-win in which there's always a way to get the money if you found an opportunity. You need to make sure you know what those strategies are. But wait a minute. I needed the down payment. I knew where to get the money. So how did I overcome lack of money? With knowledge. You see, knowledge can overcome any obstacle that you will face. Time out. That was a long chunk, wasn't it? Did you learn a lot by the chunk by the way? Okay. You're delivering that chunk. You can do it in an abbreviated version. You can stretch it out. And I really hammer on this chunk when I have all day or when I have a sufficient time. Because if you can show them that knowledge is the key to overcoming any obstacle and you get down to your clothes in which you are about to offer them an opportunity to work with you for the next year or work with a mentor for the next year, do they get total recall about all this stuff without you even mentioning it then? It all makes sense. You're selling knowledge. Knowledge will overcome any obstacle that you might have out there. Okay? Y'all ready for another chunk? It's called method of learning. Recently in our speaker training, one of the things that we cover there that I'm not covering here has to do with product development. How do you develop the product that you will be selling? Now a lot of you, you need to understand this because you are either going to develop your own product or you're going to develop a relationship with someone who has product already. Either way, aren't you developing your program? Yep. Let's say that you were selling a program that already exists, a program in which this might ring a bell for some of you, a program in which all you have to do is get someone to call a number. And then once they call that number, someone else takes over the sales process. So you don't really have to actually close the sale. All you've got to do is have them call a number and a professional salesperson that knows all these strategies and everything, then we'll walk them through the investment process. The investment process is the process that your customer will go through to invest in the highest yielding investment of their life, their own education. You need to understand what that investment is on their part. Did you know that there's one thing you can invest in that there's no law, that there's no court in the land can take away? I know a lot of you, we just learned that 50% of all marriages end a divorce. And you think, well, in a divorce, one party may get 50% of the other party. There's one thing that if I ever go through a divorce, no one can take not a percentage off, they get none of it. What is that? My knowledge. So you want to invest in something that's safe. You talk about asset security system. You talk about asset protection. You invest in your knowledge, no one can take it. And by the way, does it wear out? You get to use it over and over and over again. So if all you have to do is get someone to call a phone number and then someone else will teach them the investment process of how to invest in it, do you think it would be an easy sale on your part if all you're trying to do is get them to call a number? What do you think? What if all you're trying to do is get them to log on to a website? In other words, if all you're trying to do is that all now, you can just talk about taking action, can't you? Taking action. What would it cost you if all you did was take the time to listen to a life-changing message? How many of you think you'd take some of your time if you knew a message could change your financial status in life? Can I say a show of hands on that? Okay, then you've got them. Give them the phone number. Then they call a number and you're out of it. And then you would get a referral fee. When you look on the internet and everything, it's called an affiliate program. An affiliate program is something a lot of you that maybe all you ever sell is an affiliate program. And under the affiliate program, all you have to do is generate a lead. Generating leads, I've talked to Chamber of Commerce clubs, I've talked to investment clubs, I've talked to ladies groups, men groups, 4-H club, I've talked to church groups. I've taught these strategies for years and years and years. And in the beginning, most of my speaking was done for free. I got paid nothing. Then I found out that there are people out here that had product developed that when I'm out here speaking for free, if I would just refer people, all you've got to do is give them their phone number, tell them John called you or John told you to call. And then I started getting referral fees. People saying, hey, Joe called and he said you refer him over here. He wanted to get involved in our program and here's your affiliate referral fee. And sometimes it can be pretty heavy, folks. Let me share with you the most money that I made in speaking. I made just over, I think it was $710,000 I made one year and after all expenses were paid. Is that pretty heavy? Now if you're making more than that, you're going to think that's kind of lightweight. But if you're making less than that, you might think that would be heavyweight, right? My commission structure was 8%. I made $710,000 at 8%. Now I know of programs that I can get 10%, 12%, 14%, 15%. Now obviously what people are looking for is your track record. The more successful you become, seems like the more money you make. Not only because you make more sales, but because everything seems to be on a sliding scale. Like in the beginning, I made this percent. But then as I got a few people involved, I got a higher percent. Then as I got a few more people, pretty soon I got a higher percent of that. And it wasn't long. The fact is, every time I've been in one of those things, I would say once I set my mind to it, within a month I already had maxed out. And I've spoken with companies that I made 14%. I believe 14% is the most I've ever made as a fixed commission on what I saw. Now later on, I made the commitment to I'm going to sell my own products. Now when I sell my own products, there's a lot more involved in it. Once you start developing your own product, you write your own book, your own training, you develop your own, you'll make a higher percentage, right? You get it all. Now would it be to your advantage now, once you get your own product, to be associated with people that are accustomed to dealing in an affiliate program? Because their whole presentation is based on selling someone else's product. So in the speaking business, can you see stepping stones? That there's a process that we all go through to reach a higher income level. It's just like everything. There's that word again. What is it called? Process. Let me give you some different ones here. A process, a method, a procedure, a way. These need to become part of your vocabulary. You have no idea how many times there's always a process, a method, a way, a process. A method, a way, a process, a method, a way. And pretty soon people are understanding, I've got to learn that process. I've got to learn the method of doing something. I've got to learn the way, too. And that's what you're trying to convey here is the teaching that people have an understanding of what they've got to learn, a process, a method, a way. Now did we finish the chunk on the walls? Because knowledge is the key. It'll overcome all of your obstacles whatsoever. Okay? Let me kind of look in here and see what chunk I want to... Method of learning. Okay, that's what I was in the middle of. Method of learning. As you are developing your product, I want you, you need about three inches of lines on your paper. Let's start with a $25 product. Let's drop down a line with a $500 product. Let's drop down a line to a $1,000 product. Let's drop down a line to maybe a $5,000 product. Now a $25 product is the price represents probably what method of learning? A book. A book. Now, this is going to be revealing and eye-opening to most of you. I know. I've been there. The same thing is taught at the $500 product. It's the same thing. Now common sense. Why would anybody pay $500 if they can pay $25? The answer is method of learning. You can pay for $25, you can get my book. For $500, you can get a home study course in which I personally teach you with audio files. Your own CDs. And I explain not only the process, but I explain different variations of how it might work in your life. And what the real value is, is the fact that you get to listen to it over and over again. Because repetition is the key to learning. Method of learning. Now by the way, could you replace $500 with $250? Whatever your home study course is, you kind of plug in the number there. The difference is you teach the same thing on the CDs, your audio cassette tapes, as you did in your book. Now what could cost around $1,000? How about video tapes? Video tapes, it's the same message. You don't know how to sell video tapes? Would you like to know how to sell video tapes? Where do you learn the most? Being told or shown? Shown. You see you can get my audio tapes, they're wonderful. You can listen to them over and over again. But what I found is probably my most successful students are the ones that they follow the same procedure of repetition, but they want to see how it's done. They will like it when I tell them and then I show them. See when we're investing in the stock market, what we do there is we research the internet. And we have a computer program that actually will capture all the moves that we make on the internet. And you can see which site we go to and where we go on that site and how we gather the information. You see there are three steps in making good investments. Gather information, analyze information, and act on that information. Now as you are investing, as you are learning to invest, you can do the best job you've ever seen gathering information. You can get the best professionals available and they can analyze that information and tell you what to do. But if you don't take action, how much money would you make? None. How many times have we sat around and we just analyze things to death? It's called paralysis of analysis. You gather, you analyze, and you act. On our video tapes, you're there. You're looking over my shoulder. You're watching me do what I do. By the way, is that a good explanation of video tapes? Does it make you want the video versus the audio tapes? But it's the same information. It's method of learning. Now what's the next step up? What's your price point? $5,000. Oh. There are many options you can choose to learn. Obviously some methods of learning are more effective than others. But, you know, each of these, they may not be for everyone, but I'll share with you that there's one method of learning that has never failed. There's one method of learning that if you follow this, you will learn. You can't help but learn. And that's when a craftsman takes an apprentice and they tell him how to do it, they show him how to do it, and they make you do it. Have you heard this before? Yes. It's important to drop in that chunk, wouldn't it? Because what are you selling? An expensive training. An expensive training might be where you're there hand training people, taking them by the hand and walking them through the process of actually doing it. Because, you know, several of us said, you actually learn by doing. Well, you do learn by doing if you did it the right way. You do learn by doing more. If someone's there guiding you, if someone shows you how to do it, tells you how to do it, shows you how to do it, and then makes you do it. Now, that process is an entire week's training in which we do walk you step by step through things. And by the way, are hand-zoned trainings a more effective way to learn? Absolutely. And are they more expensive? Absolutely. Now if you're developing your product, which one do you develop? All of them. All of them. Okay? Now, if you do not have all of these products, but you want to be a speaker, doesn't it make sense to be speaking about someone's product, some person who has this entire system? See, I will not accept. When I teach speakers, I will not accept as an excuse I don't have my product developed yet. The only thing I want to know is why are you not speaking? Why aren't you out there selling someone else's program? Why aren't you making money with your knowledge? Why are you not sharing information with others? Now, who are you choosing? Make sure you choose a good product. Right? A good product.
